D2 Vision L3 Apply Reference

Adelson's Checker Shadow

toggle the connecting bars and watch two squares of identical grey finally admit they match

The visual system reads a square in shadow as a light tile dimmed by the shadow, and a square in light as a dark tile — so equal luminances split into different lightnesses.

Square A sits in light, square B in the cylinder's shadow, and they send the eye the same grey. Toggle the connecting bars: the bridge is that same grey, and it blends into both — proof the squares match.
